title: galeopsis ladanum var. filholiana
intro: Galeopsis ladanum var. filholiana, also known as a subspecies of the hemp nettle or gale of the forest, is a flowering plant that belongs to the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to the presence of essential oils such as borneol and camphor. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations. Galeopsis ladanum var. filholiana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds and utilization in traditional medicine.
